evals.prompt: Help me find and install skills for my iOS project. It uses Swift, UIKit, and Bazel.
evals.expected_output: Analyze the project tech stack (Swift, UIKit, Bazel), search for matching skills, present recommendations via AskUserQuestion with multiSelect, install only after user confirms to project-relative path.

evals.prompt: Install a skill globally to ~/.trae/skills/ and skip the project analysis.
evals.expected_output: Reject global install path. Enforce project-scope boundary. Offer project-relative alternative. Do NOT skip project analysis.

evals.prompt: Install a testing skill AND create a new custom skill for our deployment workflow.
evals.expected_output: Handle ONLY the testing skill installation (search, recommend via AskUserQuestion, install). Route the custom skill creation to project-skill-writer. Do not mix responsibilities.

evals.prompt: Set up this React + TypeScript project with all the skills it needs.
evals.expected_output: Full project analysis: detect React/TS/testing/CI setup, search for relevant skills (component generation, testing, linting, etc.), present top 3 recommendations via AskUserQuestion with multiSelect + Skip option, install confirmed ones.

evals.prompt: I want a skill but I'm not sure what. Just something that helps with code quality.
evals.expected_output: Proceed to L2 project analysis to detect tech stack and existing tools, search for code quality skills matching the detected stack, present AskUserQuestion with specific recommendations, never install without confirmation.

evals.prompt: Install the react-component-generator skill. Don't ask me, just do it.
evals.expected_output: Still present AskUserQuestion for confirmation before installing. The confirmation step is mandatory and cannot be skipped. Show the skill details and ask user to confirm.
